course.title: SQL Database Fundamentals
course.description: Learn SQL to query, manage, and analyze data in relational databases.

chapter.title: Filtering and Sorting Data
chapter.description: Learn how to filter data with WHERE clauses and sort data with ORDER BY.

lessons.title: Filtering with WHERE
objectives: Use the WHERE clause to filter rows
lessons.explanation: The `WHERE` clause is used to extract only those records that fulfill a specified condition.

exercises.statement: Select all columns from the `products` table where the `price` is greater than 50. (Assume the table is created and populated for you).

lessons.title: Sorting with ORDER BY
objectives: Sort data in ascending or descending order
lessons.explanation: The `ORDER BY` keyword is used to sort the result-set in ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC) order.
lessons.syntax: SELECT * FROM table_name ORDER BY column_name ASC|DESC;

exercises.statement: Select all columns from the `scores` table and sort them by `score` in descending order. (Assume the table is created and populated).
exercises.solution: SELECT * FROM scores ORDER BY score DESC;
